page: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls within the average range, multi-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching a top-tier 7543.56 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 349.82 GB/sec. Passmark testing ranks the CPU score, extended instructions, encryption, and physics capabilities in the top 10%. However, the Passmark memory score itself is average, indicating a potential tradeoff between raw bandwidth and latency-sensitive memory operations.

This server does not include local storage, requiring external Premium Storage for data persistence. It is highly capable in database operations, achieving top-tier performance in Passmark database benchmarks and 160.72 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. Additionally, the presence of AMX instructions enables top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, such as 40.01 tokens per second on a 70B model, though text generation speeds remain average. The server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, large-scale Redis deployments, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 768.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). While single-core performance is average, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks rank in the top tier, with cached read bandwidth reaching 7543.56 GB/sec. The server lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable. It delivers top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and LLM prompt processing. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth. It is designed for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and top-tier database and multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs, massive memory bandwidth, and advanced matrix extensions for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory.
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7543.56 GB/sec.
Intel AMX support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and database benchmarks.
No local storage included; relies on external Premium Storage.
Suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and LLM prompt processing.
